findings.description: The entire change is predicated on PR #211 (extend-kanban-viz-prs-proposals) being in the codebase, but it is NOT merged and NOT an ancestor of this branch. Verified: this branch forked from main@6e0431b; `git merge-base --is-ancestor 281e95c HEAD` returns false; PR #211's tip is reachable ONLY from the `openspec/extend-kanban-viz-prs-proposals` branch. Consequently every file the plan targets is absent from the base: SPA has `Card.tsx`/`VendorSwimlanes.tsx`/`useCoordinator.ts` (NOT `PRCardView.tsx`, `ProposalCardView.tsx`, `SourceSwimlanes.tsx`, `useBoardCards.ts`/`clusterBoardCards`); `coordinator-types.ts` defines only `Issue` (NOT `IssueCard`/`PRCard`/`ProposalCard`/`BoardCard`); coordinator has `github_coordination.py` (NOT `github_prs_api.py` or `openspec_proposals_api.py`); `_get_repo_root()` does not exist; `saved-view.json` has `change_ids`/`vendors` filters (NOT `pr_origins`/`hidden_rows`). No artifact (proposal, design, tasks, work-packages) declares #211 as a blocking prerequisite, merge-order constraint, or rebase target — the proposal instead states '#211 just shipped' (proposal.md:212), implying it is merged. The plan is not implementable as written until #211 lands.

This change's contracts/generated/types.ts imports PR #211 types via a relative path into the SIBLING change directory: `from "../../../extend-kanban-viz-prs-proposals/contracts/generated/types"` (lines 16-40). This path breaks the moment PR #211 is archived, because the archive step moves it to `openspec/changes/archive/<date>-extend-kanban-viz-prs-proposals/...`. It also can't be resolved by the SPA at all (tsconfig `include: ["src"]`), so the file is unusable as a real import target from the app. Cross-change directory imports are not a stable contract boundary.

Proposal and design disagree on the empty-default behavior of OPENSPEC_SOURCES. Proposal says empty default = 'current single-checkout behavior' (proposal.md:37, 226), implying ProposalCard.repo stays effectively unchanged/null. Design D1 (design.md:33) + tasks.md:64 (task 4.4, marked CRITICAL) instead synthesize an implicit `local:.` source that DERIVES a non-null repo from `git remote get-url origin`. These are materially different defaults. Under the design's rule, the D3 'bare change_id fallback when every member has repo:null' (proposal.md:191, design.md:54-56) becomes near-dead code for proposals, since ProposalCard.repo is never null. Reconcile the proposal's stated default with the design's implicit-local-source rule.

derive_local_repo's basename fallback produces a repo value with NO slash (e.g. 'agentic-coding-tools'), but every other consumer assumes `<owner>/<repo>` form: the hidden_repos saved-view regex is `^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$` (tasks.md:78, requires a slash), RepoBadge hover advertises 'the full <owner>/<repo>', and namespaced cluster keys are `<repo>/<change-id>`. A local source lacking an origin remote therefore yields a repo that (a) cannot be hidden via hidden_repos because it fails the schema regex, and (b) renders/clusters inconsistently. Either normalize the basename fallback into an owner/repo-shaped value (e.g. `local/<basename>`) or relax the regex and document the single-segment case.

getClusterKey pseudocode (design.md:231-235) computes `baseId = card.change_id ?? card.change_id_namespaced ?? null` then returns `${card.repo}/${baseId}`. But change_id_namespaced is ALREADY `<repo>/<change-id>`. When change_id is null and change_id_namespaced is populated, the result is a doubled namespace `repo/repo/change-id`, producing a key that won't match sibling cards keyed off bare change_id. The fallback chain should extract the bare change-id component (e.g. strip the leading `<repo>/` from change_id_namespaced), not concatenate the already-namespaced value.

Design states the SPA 're-exports from' the in-change contracts/generated/types.ts (design.md:210), but elsewhere (design.md:9, tasks.md:100 task 7.2) correctly states the SPA cannot import outside `apps/kanban-viz/src/` (tsconfig `include: ["src"]`), so the real flow is a manually-synced runtime copy in `coordinator-types.ts` — NOT a re-export. The two descriptions are incompatible. Pick one: the contracts file is a spec-only artifact and the SPA hand-maintains its copy (consistent with the rest of the plan), so remove/reword the 're-exports from there' claim at design.md:210.

Tasks assert regression baselines that don't exist in the branch base because PR #211 isn't merged: task 8.4 'SPA test suite green (138 PR #211 tests + new tests)', task 4.5 'full coordinator test suite (including PR #211 tests, to verify no regression)', task 7.4/7.5 'existing PR #211 cluster tests at apps/kanban-viz/src/hooks/__tests__/useBoardCards.test.ts'. None of these files/tests are present in the tree. The specific count '138' cannot be validated. These checkpoints will mislead an implementer about what 'green' means. (Root cause is R1-001; resolving the merge-order dependency fixes these, but the hardcoded count should be removed or made conditional.)

The proposal's 'Open Questions for Implementation' (proposal.md:258-283) lists repo: label canonicalization, local-source repo derivation, and GitHub rate-limit batching as OPEN, but the design has already RESOLVED all three: D4 fixes lowercase normalization (design.md:68), R2 fixes origin-first-then-basename (design.md:269-271), and D7 fixes the REST budget cap with GraphQL deferred (design.md:92-100). Reading the proposal alone, these appear unresolved. Either trim them from the proposal's open-questions list or annotate them as 'resolved in design (D4/R2/D7)'.

Cache freshness labeling is slightly misleading. Design D2 (design.md:46-48) treats local sources as 'always live in this model', but local data is walked at boot and then served from cache until the next boot or ?refresh — it is not re-walked per request. Labeling boot-cached filesystem data as 'live' (and feeding it into the `source: live|cache|mixed` signal) understates staleness when the underlying checkout changes between refreshes. Consider documenting that 'live' for local means 'as-of-last-walk' and that cache_age_seconds should reflect the local walk time too.
